summary: Iteration 4 bug-fix verification: HTTP Range support for /api/files/{filename} + 5 frontend fixes for playback reset & seek slider. Added 6 new pytest cases (TestRangeRequests) covering 206/416/Accept-Ranges/open-ended ranges/invalid units. All 21 backend tests pass (15 prior + 6 new). curl-verified on localhost:8001 AND through the public Cloudflare URL — Range headers are forwarded correctly. Frontend E2E confirms: seek slider is enabled (data-disabled=None) when track loaded, play button click no longer resets audio.currentTime to 0 (advanced 0.96s -> 3.36s over 2.5s wall-clock), and arrow-key seek on slider advances currentTime by ~1s. No regressions.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py serve_file: streaming response opens the file inside the generator (good — file handle closes when generator exits or client disconnects). Consider adding a Content-Disposition header if you ever serve covers with non-ASCII names; for now filenames are server-generated (file_id) so this is fine.

server.py serve_file: When the parsed range is technically valid syntactically but end < start, the code raises ValueError and returns 416 — correct per RFC 7233. Also handles start<0 and end>=file_size as 416. Good defensive parsing.

Room.jsx: optimistic stateMetaRef update in handleSeekCommit is the correct fix; pre-fix, the 200ms tick computed `target` from a stale `play_started_at` and snapped audio back, making seek appear broken. Verified via E2E that currentTime advances continuously.
test_credentials: No auth — any non-empty name accepted
seed_data_creation: Tests create ephemeral rooms via POST /api/rooms and upload synthetic MP3 (ID3v2 + a few MPEG frames). Cleanup is automatic via WS-disconnect cleanup task.
context_for_next_testing_agent: TestRangeRequests class in test_syncplay.py covers all HTTP Range edge cases (no-range/206 partial/300 bytes/open-ended/416 beyond-EOF/416 invalid-unit). The Cloudflare proxy on this preview env DOES forward Range headers (verified — public URL returns 206 + Content-Range), so future tests don't need to special-case localhost. AudioPlayer seek slider uses `disabled={!track}` (audioReady requirement removed in iteration 4 fix); confirmed via DOM `data-disabled=None` when a track is loaded.
rca of the issue: Pre-fix: FastAPI's FileResponse ignored Range headers and always returned 200 with full file. Chrome <audio> seek triggers a Range request and on 200-full-file falls back to in-memory buffering with broken currentTime semantics — manifested as 'plays then resets to 0 and pauses' and 'seek slider does nothing'. Fix: parse Range header manually, return 206 Partial Content with Accept-Ranges/Content-Range/Content-Length using StreamingResponse. Frontend complementary fixes (loadedSrcRef, optimistic state in handleSeekCommit/togglePlay/handleAudioEnded, removed audioReady gate on slider) prevent the 200ms tick from snapping back to a stale server position before the WS roundtrip completes.
